prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `7acdf6d3`.
codex_response:
Implemented `7acdf6d3` in [task_7acdf6d3](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7acdf6d3). The rule is: count all donor-color cells, find the other color’s connected component whose row-wise horizontal interior gaps have exactly that count, erase the donor cells, and fill only those gaps with the donor color. I rejected the Opus hint as incomplete and documented that in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7acdf6d3/notes.md); Sonnet had no entry. No shared primitive was added, only a task-local helper in [helpers.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7acdf6d3/helpers.py), used by both [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7acdf6d3/verifier.py) and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7acdf6d3/generator.py).

The generator explicitly builds sparse square grids with two outline-like recipient components from the same family seen in the originals and a separate donor object whose cell budget matches exactly one target component. Validation passed end to end: official verifier `3/3`, generated verifier `1000/1000`, and the built artifacts look on-family in preview inspection.
